—The Origin

Dallas had never had its own spirit. Born and raised in the city, I wanted to change that — so in 2016, my cofounder and I built Silver Nickel Distillery from the ground up. A 7,200 sq ft production facility, licensed from scratch, with copper pot stills and the singular mission of creating Dallas' first-ever branded vodka. No playbook. No category precedent. Just a city worth naming a spirit after.

The full lineup — Original, Lemon, Oro Blanco (Grapefruit)

Trade event showcase — all three SKUs, statewide rollout

—My Role

Co-distiller, brand creator, production lead, marketer, and salesperson — often all in the same day. I conceived the product line, designed all branding and packaging, navigated federal and state labeling regulations, built out the go-to-market strategy, and personally sold into the largest liquor chains in Texas.

—What Got Built
Brand IdentityFull visual system — logo, bottle design, packaging, collateral, and event assets
Product LineThree flavors: Original, Lemon, and Oro Blanco (grapefruit) — 10x pot-still distilled
Distribution DealStatewide contract with Republic National Distributing Co. — 2nd largest in the U.S.
Marketing & EventsConsumer promos, social campaigns, retail tastings, parades, and sponsored events
Contract DistillingB2B production of vodka, gin, and whiskey for undisclosed third-party brands
AcquisitionSilver Nickel Distillery acquired by new proprietor, July 2021
—Timeline
2016
Co-founded Silver Nickel Distillery. Built facility from scratch.
2017
Katy Trail Vodka Original launched in Texas.
2018
Oro Blanco + Lemon added. RNDC statewide distribution secured.
2019–20
Expanded to Colorado. Contract distilling revenue added.
2021
Exit. Distillery acquired by new proprietor.

Street activation with the branded cargo bike
